---
title: "401_k_est"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-05-22"
output: html_document
---
```{r, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!require("OutcomeWeights")) install.packages("OutcomeWeights", dependencies = TRUE); library(OutcomeWeights)
if (!require("hdm")) install.packages("hdm", dependencies = TRUE); library(hdm)
if (!require("grf")) install.packages("grf", dependencies = TRUE); library(grf)
if (!require("cobalt")) install.packages("cobalt", dependencies = TRUE); library(cobalt)
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse", dependencies = TRUE); library(tidyverse)
if (!require("viridis")) install.packages("viridis", dependencies = TRUE); library(viridis)
if (!require("gridExtra")) install.packages("gridExtra", dependencies = TRUE); library(gridExtra)
if (!require("AER")) install.packages("AER", dependencies = TRUE); library(AER)
if (!require("sandwich")) install.packages("sandwich", dependencies = TRUE); library(sandwich)
if (!require("lmtest")) install.packages("lmtest", dependencies = TRUE); library(lmtest)


data(pension) # Find variable description if you type ?pension in console
cat(sprintf("Sample size: N = %d\n", nrow(pension)))
# Treatment
D <- pension$p401
Z <- pension$e401
Y <- pension$net_tfa

# Design matrix WITH intercept (for kappa/propensity score functions)
X_401_kappa <- model.matrix(
  ~ age + db + educ + fsize + hown + inc + male + marr + pira + twoearn,
  data = pension
)

colnames(X_401_kappa) <- c("(Intercept)", "age", "db", "educ", "fsize", 
                            "hown", "inc", "male", "marr", "pira", "twoearn")

# Design matrix WITHOUT intercept (for ivreg)
X_401_df <- as.data.frame(X_401_kappa[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# Pretty labels kept separately — for plots/tables only
var_nm <- c("Age", "Benefit pension", "Education", "Family size", 
            "Home owner", "Income", "Male", "Married", "IRA", "Two earners")
# Design matrix WITHOUT intercept (for ivreg)
X_401_df <- as.data.frame(X_401_kappa[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# for the next step alreading reading in all functions I need 
source("/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/showing/functions_card.R")


```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# estimating the kappa estimators 
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("Replicating the Kappa Estimators ")
cat("==============================================================================\n")


# this is to run every column
run_column <- function(Y, Z, D, X_kappa, X_df, label) {
  cat(sprintf("  Computing column: %s\n", label))

  # 2SLS
  tsls      <- run_2sls(Y, D, Z, X_df, endog_name = "D")

  # Kappa estimators (point estimates + M-estimation SEs)
  kappa_res <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y, Z, D, X_kappa)

  list(tsls = tsls, kappa = kappa_res)
}



# compuing propensity score and maximum likelihood
cat("\nEstimating propensity scores...\n")
p_ml_401 <- logit_mle(Z, X_401_kappa)
p_cb_401 <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_401_kappa)
cat("  Done.\n")



# for some collega attendance parted in Card and Kitagawa
cat("\n--- Treatment assigned ---\n")

# Col 1: somecol, cents, Card spec
col1 <- run_column(Y, Z, D, X_401_kappa, X_401_df, "col1: Treatment")

```


```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# TABLE: Kappa estimators for 401(k) — participation on net total financial assets
# ==============================================================================

print_table_401k <- function(col1) {
  cat("\n")
  cat("==============================================================================\n")
  cat("Kappa Estimators: Effect of 401(k) Participation on Net Financial Assets\n")
  cat("Instrument: 401(k) eligibility (e401)\n")
  cat("Treatment:  401(k) participation (p401)\n")
  cat("Outcome:    Net total financial assets (net_tfa, in USD)\n")
  cat("==============================================================================\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n", "", "(1)"))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n", "Treatment:", "p401"))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n", "Outcome:",   "net_tfa"))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n", "Spec:",      "pension covariates"))
  cat(strrep("-", 35), "\n")

  # Panel A: 2SLS
  cat("\nPanel A: 2SLS\n")
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", "2SLS",     col1$tsls$coef))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", "",          col1$tsls$se))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n",   "",          "[SE]"))

  # Panel B: Normalized kappa estimators
  cat("\nPanel B: Normalized kappa estimators\n")
  for (est in c("tau_cb_u", "tau_ml_u", "tau_ml_a10")) {
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", est, col1$kappa$estimates[est]))
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", "",  col1$kappa$se[est]))
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n",   "",  "[SE]"))
  }

  # Panel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators
  cat("\nPanel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators\n")
  for (est in c("tau_ml_a", "tau_ml_t", "tau_ml_a0")) {
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", est, col1$kappa$estimates[est]))
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10.3f\n", "",  col1$kappa$se[est]))
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %10s\n",   "",  "[SE]"))
  }

  cat(strrep("-", 35), "\n")
  cat("Notes: SE = M-estimation sandwich standard errors.\n")
  cat("       Panel B estimators are translation invariant (sum of weights ~ 0).\n")
  cat("       Panel C estimators are NOT translation invariant.\n")
}

print_table_401k(col1)

```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SCALE-EQUIVARIANCE CHECK (401k analogue of the Card translation-invariance check)
# ==============================================================================
# In Card: Y_cents = Y_dollars + log(100)  →  additive shift  →  tests sum(w) = 0
# In 401k: Y_thousands = Y_dollars / 1000  →  scale shift     →  tests tau_hat
#          scales proportionally: tau_thousands = tau_dollars / 1000
#
# All estimators SHOULD pass this — it is a basic sanity check.
# If an estimator fails, something is numerically wrong with that implementation.
# This also gives you a clean first look at the weight sums for your design.
# ==============================================================================

k_scale <- 1000   # dollars -> thousands

cat("\n\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("SCALE-EQUIVARIANCE CHECK\n")
cat("Demonstrates: all estimators scale correctly when Y switches from\n")
cat("dollars to thousands of dollars (net_tfa / 1000).\n")
cat("tau_thousands should equal tau_dollars / 1000 for all estimators.\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")

Y_dol  <- Y                 # net_tfa in dollars (original)
Y_thou <- Y / k_scale       # net_tfa in thousands of dollars

scale_check <- function(X_kappa, spec_label) {
  p_ml  <- logit_mle(Z, X_kappa)
  p_cb  <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_kappa)
  kw_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  kw_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)

  cat(sprintf("\n=== %s ===\n", spec_label))
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  %12s  %12s  %12s  %10s\n",
              "Estimator", "Dollars", "Thousands", "Ratio(act)", "Match?"))
  cat(strrep("-", 72), "\n")

  # w removed from signature — ratio check doesn't need it
  pr <- function(name, est_dol, est_thou) {
    ratio_act <- est_dol / est_thou        # should equal k_scale = 1000
    ok <- isTRUE(all.equal(ratio_act, k_scale, tolerance = 1e-4))
    cat(sprintf("%-18s  %12.2f  %12.4f  %12.4f  %10s\n",
                name, est_dol, est_thou, ratio_act,
                if (ok) "TRUE" else "FALSE"))
  }

  # Normalized
  pr("tau_cb_u",
     tau_u(Y_dol, Z, D, p_cb),    tau_u(Y_thou, Z, D, p_cb))
  pr("tau_ml_u",
     tau_u(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml),    tau_u(Y_thou, Z, D, p_ml))
  pr("tau_ml_a10",
     tau_a10(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml),  tau_a10(Y_thou, Z, D, p_ml))

  # Unnormalized
  pr("tau_ml_a",
     tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a"),  tau_unnorm(Y_thou, Z, D, p_ml, "a"))
  pr("tau_ml_t",
     tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a1"), tau_unnorm(Y_thou, Z, D, p_ml, "a1"))
  pr("tau_ml_a0",
     tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"), tau_unnorm(Y_thou, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"))

  # Sum of weights — printed separately, doesn't belong inside pr()
  cat(sprintf("\n  Sum of weights (should be ~0 for normalized estimators):\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f\n", "tau_cb_u",   sum(kw_cb$w_u)))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f\n", "tau_ml_u",   sum(kw_ml$w_u)))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f\n", "tau_ml_a10", sum(kw_ml$w_a10)))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f  [NOT ~0]\n", "tau_ml_a",  sum(kw_ml$w_a)))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f  [NOT ~0]\n", "tau_ml_t",  sum(kw_ml$w_a1)))
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  sum(w) = %10.6f  [NOT ~0]\n", "tau_ml_a0", sum(kw_ml$w_a0)))
}

scale_check(X_401_kappa, "p401 — pension covariates")


```


```{r}



cat("\n\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS TABLE\n")
cat("401(k): instrument = e401, treatment = p401, outcome = net_tfa\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")

diag_block_401k <- function(X_kappa, label) {
  p_ml  <- logit_mle(Z, X_kappa)
  p_cb  <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_kappa)
  kw_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  kw_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)

  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s ---\n", label))
  tab <- rbind(
    weight_diag(kw_cb$w_u,   "tau_cb_u"),
    weight_diag(kw_ml$w_u,   "tau_ml_u"),
    weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a10, "tau_ml_a10"),
    weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a,   "tau_ml_a"),
    weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a1,  "tau_ml_t"),
    weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a0,  "tau_ml_a0")
  )
  print(tab, row.names = FALSE)

  # additionally: algebraic identity check (sum(w*Y) == tau_hat)
  cat("\n  Algebraic identity check: sum(w * Y) == tau_hat\n")
  checks <- c(
    tau_cb_u   = check_weight_identity(kw_cb$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_cb)),
    tau_ml_u   = check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    tau_ml_a10 = check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a10, Y, tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    tau_ml_a   = check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a,   Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a")),
    tau_ml_t   = check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a1,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a1")),
    tau_ml_a0  = check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a0,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"))
  )
  for (nm in names(checks)) {
    cat(sprintf("  %-14s  %s\n", nm, if (checks[nm]) "TRUE" else "FALSE"))
  }
}

diag_block_401k(X_401_kappa, "p401 — pension covariates")






```
